title: Sutera argentea
taxonomy: Sutera argentea is a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. It belongs to the genus Sutera, which is a part of the Plantaginaceae family. The Plantaginaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the plantain family. This family is further classified under the order Lamiales, which includes many other flowering plant families. Sutera argentea is native to Australia and is also known as the silver rain.

Its leaves and stems are said to possess anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it potentially useful for wound care and skin issues.
